national_expectations.summary: After a long absence from the World Cup, simply reaching the knockout rounds is treated as a milestone for this Haaland-and-Odegaard generation, widely framed as the strongest in Norwegian history. Coverage describes a country allowing itself to dream big while treating the Ivory Coast tie as a genuinely even, winnable match rather than a formality, with the captain openly saying it is permitted to dream large and go for it.

national_mood.summary: Buoyant and optimistic but measured. Norway navigated early tournament nerves to advance, and the manager's relief was visible after the decisive group win. The press conveys a rested, hungry squad and a nation enjoying a long-awaited return, tempered by respect for an Ivory Coast side that the manager describes as a coin-flip opponent requiring Norway's best.

manager_situation.summary: Stale Solbakken stands on solid ground, having guided Norway back to the global stage and out of the group. He was widely backed for boldly rotating heavily for the dead-rubber final group match, resting Haaland and other starters to keep his squad fresh for the knockout. He has publicly framed Ivory Coast as physically among the tournament's best and the tie as an even, Senegal-like contest, signalling a respectful, hungry approach rather than complacency.

historical_context.summary: Norway reached this World Cup after a generation away from the finals, their first appearance since 1998. The qualifying run was dominant and included statement wins over Italy, and the campaign has been built around Haaland's prolific scoring and Odegaard's playmaking, fuelling talk of a potential golden generation.

dominant_storylines.storyline: Haaland's tournament: after a flying start of four goals across the two opening group wins, the talisman was deliberately rested for the dead-rubber final group match and is now the central question for the knockout - whether he rediscovers his clinical edge on the big stage to fire Norway through.

The big rotation gamble: with qualification already secured, Solbakken made sweeping changes for the final group game - resting Haaland, the captain and the defence - prioritising fresh legs for the knockout over chasing top spot. The full-strength France side duly won, sending Norway to face Ivory Coast, and the press has largely endorsed the trade-off.

A coin-flip knockout against Ivory Coast: the manager and Norwegian press frame the Dallas tie as an even, winnable contest he likens to Norway's tight group test against Senegal, with the Nusa-versus-Diomande RB Leipzig club-team-mate subplot on the right flank flagged as a potentially pivotal tactical battle.

attributed_quotes.quote: It is allowed to dream big, and we are going to go for it.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Det er lov a dromme stort, og vi skal kline til.
attributed_quotes.context: The captain speaking after the group-stage win over Senegal that sent Norway through, as the team looked ahead to the knockout rounds.

attributed_quotes.quote: We are very happy having six points. We should not become too greedy.
attributed_quotes.context: Explaining his decision to rotate heavily and rest Haaland and other starters for the final group match, with qualification already secured.

local_availability_reporting.detail: Reported as rested rather than injured for the final group match against France; previews expect him to return at right-back for the knockout tie.

gaps: No official Norwegian Football Federation (NFF) or FIFA statement was sourced for national expectations, mood, the manager's standing, or historical context; all sections rest on media/press (discovery tier).

The detail that Solbakken personally featured in Norway's last (1998) World Cup squad could not be verified from a cited source and was removed; only Norway's 1998 last-appearance is press-confirmed (Yahoo Sports).

No confirmed injury or suspension was reported for Norway for the R32 tie; previews state Ryerson was rested (not injured) and expected to return, so it is logged as reported/uncertain availability only.

The full verbatim Solbakken Norwegian-language quote calling Ivory Coast 'physically one of the best teams' and a '50/50' game was located at NRK/SI but is summarised in the storyline/manager blocks rather than carried as a third attributed quote (cap of two).
DB cross-check confirmed: press corroborates Group I results Iraq 4-1 W, Senegal 3-2 W, France 4-1 L (W2 L1, 2nd behind France), consistent with DB FACTS.
